title: Slow Burner

chord_progression.theory_notes: E natural minor: E F# G A B C D. The progression i→v→bVI is one of the most emotionally resonant in minor key music. Em (root, dark) → Bm (5th, tension) → C major (major chord built on the flat-6th) gives a dramatic lift that defines IC's sound. The C major (bVI) is borrowed from the parallel major scale — it's a moment of brightness inside the darkness. The v chord (Bm) is minor rather than the classical V (B major), giving a softer, more melancholic tension than hard UK garage. This is deeply Interplanetary Criminal: emotional, brooding, with that one beam of light in the C major.

structure.description: Piano and organ only — no vocal, no beat. Establishes the emotional chord loop.
Vocal enters (bar 14). Clap on 2+4 and open hat enter (bar 11). Perc Akcent audio loop adds texture. Still no kick — pure atmosphere.
Kick (C4) and bass enter together. Full arrangement: kick + clap + open hat + bass + piano + organ. Shaker audio adds groove.
Tambourine enters at bar 47 — adds rhythmic energy on top of the existing groove. Bass continues same 4-bar pattern.
Kick/clap/hat drop out. Break Bass enters with a busier, more fragmented version of the bass riff. Organ sustains the chord context. Breaks audio sample (bars 71–79) adds textural percussion.
Clock Fx loop (bars 79–94) creates a ticking, minimal backdrop. Piano returns. Impact audio hits on downbeats. Pure tension — no kick.
Kick and full arrangement return. Bass variation (clip 3) with additional 16th-note articulations and higher register accents (E5, G5, E6).
Same as Drop 3. Perc Akcent and Shaker continue through final section.

tracks.name: Clap
tracks.role: Clap on beats 2 and 4 (positions 1.0, 3.0 per bar), C4, velocity 100

tracks.name: Open Hat
tracks.role: Open hat on every off-beat (0.5, 1.5, 2.5, 3.5), C4, velocity 100 — UK garage signature

tracks.name: Crash Delayed
tracks.role: Crash hits every 2 bars at major section transitions

tracks.name: Tambourine
tracks.role: Enters at Drop 2 (bar 47) for added hi-frequency groove

tracks.name: Bass
tracks.role: Main melodic bass — walks E minor pentatonic (E4→G4→C5→E5→A4→G4) over 4-bar phrase. Bars 31–62, 97–128.

tracks.name: Break Bass
tracks.role: Busier bass variation in breakdown (bars 63–71) — more fragmented 16th-note pattern

tracks.name: 90s Piano
tracks.role: Main chord instrument — plays Em→Bm→C→Bm loop as block chords. Present bars 11–30, 47–62, 79–94, 105–128.

tracks.name: Organ
tracks.role: Single-note organ melody over the chord loop. E4→G4→C5→A4→G4 melodic line. Bars 11–30, 63–79.

tracks.name: Laser Fx
tracks.role: Laser FX stabs in breakdown (bars 75–77, 91–93) — tension device

tracks.name: Perc Akcent
tracks.role: Percussive audio loop, adds groove texture throughout drops

tracks.name: Shaker
tracks.role: Shaker groove loop, enters with Drop 1 (bar 31)

tracks.name: Clock Fx
tracks.role: Ticking/clock texture loop in atmospheric break (bars 79–94)

tracks.name: Impact
tracks.role: Impact hit on downbeats through drops and break

bass_pattern.key_notes: E minor pentatonic: E, G, A, B (implied), C (bVI colour), E (octave)
bass_pattern.description: The bass plays a 4-bar (16-beat) melodic loop — not a simple root pattern but a walking melody. E4 on the downbeat, rises through G4→C5 over bar 2, peaks at E5 then descends through A4→G4 back to E4. It outlines the chord changes (E root over Em, C5 accent over Cmaj) while staying inside E minor pentatonic.

key_techniques: i→v→bVI chord loop — Em→Bm→C — the major VI chord inside minor creates that IC emotional 'lift'
4-on-the-floor kick with open hat on every off-beat (not 2-step) — closer to house than garage
Piano enters 20 bars before the kick — establishes chords first, beat second
No kick in breakdown for 35 bars (bars 63–96) — maximum tension through restraint
Break Bass variation in breakdown uses busier 16th-note runs vs. the clean main bass
Clock Fx loop in atmospheric break — textural tension without percussion
Tambourine as energy upgrade between drop 1 and drop 2 (enters bar 47)
theory_notes: Slow Burner is built on E natural minor at 136 BPM. The chord progression Em→Bm→C (i→v→bVI) runs as a looping 4-bar phrase throughout the track. Analytically: the v chord (Bm, not B major) keeps the harmonic tension 'soft' — you're never resolving to a dominant V. The real emotional weight is in the C major (bVI), which borrows from the parallel E major scale. It sounds like a chord that 'doesn't belong' but creates an achingly beautiful lift. Compare to MPH Raw which has zero chords — IC Slow Burner is the melodic, emotional end of the UK garage spectrum. Both tracks are 136 BPM; the difference is entirely in harmonic content.
vs_mph_raw: MPH Raw: no chords, phrygian tension, rhythmic aggression. IC Slow Burner: beautiful minor chord loop, emotional atmosphere, 35-bar breakdowns. Same BPM (136), completely different emotional worlds. MPH = floor energy, IC = feelings.

elements_to_transplant: i→v→bVI chord loop (transpose to any minor key — e.g. Am→Em→F in A minor)
Open hat on every off-beat for driving 4/4 feel
20+ bar chord-only intro before kick drops
Break Bass technique: vary bass density/rhythm in breakdown vs. drops
Atmospheric break with texture loop instead of drums
